## Safety & approval

- Separate facts, assumptions, and recommendations.
- Do not invent private data, prices, policies, laws, medical facts, or platform rules.
- Before any irreversible action, produce a review table and wait for the user to say `apply`, `send`, `publish`, or another explicit approval.
- YouTube is strictly read-only - no comment replies, no metadata edits, no publishing actions from this playbook.
- Refreshing an existing brief page must never overwrite human-authored blocks; updates are appended and marked.

## Output format

**Video** - resolved ID, title, publish date, link.

**Handoff brief** (purpose-shaped; repurposing default shown)

- **Chapters & structure** - | Timestamp | Chapter | Note |
- **Description** - preserved verbatim with links
- **Audience digest** - question clusters with counts, verbatim quotes, praise/criticism split
- **Angles & gaps** - what viewers asked that the video didn't answer
- **Assets** - thumbnail reference, tags, related links from the description

**Proposed write** - destination, page title, properties, existing-page check result. *(approval checkpoint)*

**Run summary**

| Step | App | Produced | Status |

## Do

- Preserve timestamps, links, and comment quotes verbatim - the recipient needs exact strings, not summaries
- Cluster repeated audience questions with counts; that is the signal repurposers act on
- Shape the section order to the stated purpose instead of one fixed layout
- Append to refreshed pages; never clobber human edits

## Don't

- Don't reply to comments or touch video metadata - the platform side is read-only
- Don't fabricate comment sentiment or paraphrase quotes into something no viewer said
- Don't create a second brief page when one matches the video ID - refresh it
- Don't build the brief on an unconfirmed video resolution

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] The video was resolved and confirmed from a live read before the pull
- [ ] Timestamps, description links, and quotes are verbatim from the connector reads
- [ ] The existing-page check ran before any creation, and refreshes preserved human blocks
- [ ] The only write was the approved, verified Notion page
- [ ] Run summary table shows step, app, produced artifact, status for the whole run

description: >

# /automation-connector-playbooks-youtube-notion-failure-check

Diagnose workflow failures for video and Notion workflows: a connector troubleshooting report. Produces a copy-ready failure report. Triggers: "video and Notion workflows failure-check", /automation-connector-playbooks-youtube-notion-failure-check.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this when the content pipeline that runs through Notion has stopped matching the channel that runs on YouTube: pages sit in "Scheduled" past their planned date with no video live, videos went up that the tracker never heard about, a "Published" page links to a video that is now private or deleted, or the tracker's metadata (titles, dates) has drifted from the platform's. This check reads both sides, diffs them by video ID and planned date, and reports every stalled or broken handoff in the plan→publish→track loop - read-only, with each finding carrying evidence from both apps and a proposed repair for a separate run or a human.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; ask at most one question if the missing detail changes the answer.

- The Notion tracker database and its property map: video URL/ID property, status property and its vocabulary (which values mean planned / scheduled / published), planned-date property
- The channel/uploads scope and the check window (default: 30 days back for uploads, plus all tracker pages in scheduled/pre-publish statuses regardless of age)
- Grace period: how many days past a planned date counts as stalled rather than merely rescheduled (default 3)
- Which metadata fields are expected to match between tracker and platform (title, publish date) vs which legitimately differ (working titles)
- Known exceptions: unlisted/member-only videos, seasonal pauses, pages intentionally parked

## Workflow

1. **Read the tracker.** All pages with a video ID set, plus all pages in pre-publish statuses: page ID, status, planned date, linked video ID/URL, title, last-edited time.
2. **Read the channel.** Uploads in the window: video ID, title, publish date/time, and privacy/availability as far as the connector exposes it.
3. **Diff on video ID** both directions, and on planned date for pages that have no ID yet (the not-yet-linked plan rows).
4. **Sweep plan→publish stalls:** pages in scheduled/production statuses whose planned date passed the grace period with no linked video live - the classic silent stall; rank by days overdue. Distinguish "no video exists at all" from "video exists but the page was never linked" (search uploads by title similarity for the latter, marked as weak-key evidence).
5. **Sweep publish→track gaps:** live uploads with no tracker page (the pipeline's record-keeping failed - these erode every future report built on the tracker), and pages whose status never advanced to published although their linked video is live (status lag, with the lag duration).
6. **Sweep integrity breaks:** published pages whose linked video is unavailable (private/deleted/wrong ID - quote what the connector returns verbatim), and metadata drift where tracker title/date disagree with the platform's for fields the user said must match.
7. **Classify, rank, and propose repairs:** stalls (needs a human decision: reschedule or unblock), untracked publishes and status lag (hand to the approval-queue playbook to sync), integrity breaks (needs investigation - never propose deleting the page or the link automatically). Every finding carries page link + video ID + both-sides dates, or is labeled a hypothesis. This run writes nothing in either app.

**Re-run behavior.** Read-only, naturally idempotent. Findings keyed by video ID (or page ID for unlinked plans) + failure mode, so consecutive runs diff cleanly and carried-over stalls show their growing age.

**Fallbacks.** If YouTube is unavailable, run the tracker-side sweep alone (overdue scheduled pages, missing IDs) and state that publish-side verification is unchecked. If Notion is unavailable, list the window's uploads inline so the user can eyeball their tracker, and label the run one-sided. Never present a one-sided sweep as the full check.

## Connector access (required)

This skill cannot run from text alone. It must call live integrations.

1. Confirm each required app is enabled and authenticated in the current AI tool.
2. Discover the exact available connector tools first; do not assume the example names exist.
3. Use read-only calls before proposing writes. For sends, deletes, publishes, refunds, or updates, show a review table and wait for explicit approval.

### Required integrations

- **@youtube** - required live workspace access. Connector names and tool names vary by AI tool; discover available tools before calling anything. MCP key hint: `youtube`.
- **@notion** - required live workspace access. Connector names and tool names vary by AI tool; discover available tools before calling anything. MCP key hint: `notion`.

### If a connector is missing

Stop and tell the user which app is missing. Give setup steps for their tool, then ask them to retry after authentication. Do not substitute generic web search for private workspace data.

## Safety & approval

- Separate facts, assumptions, and recommendations.
- Do not invent private data, prices, policies, laws, medical facts, or platform rules.
- Before any irreversible action, produce a review table and wait for the user to say `apply`, `send`, `publish`, or another explicit approval.
- This playbook is read-only end to end in both apps; every repair it proposes executes only through a separately approved run.
- Unavailable-video findings quote the connector's response verbatim and never speculate about why a video is gone.

## Output format

**Scope** - database + property map, channel/window, grace period, exceptions honored.

**Failure report**

| # | Failure mode (stall / untracked publish / status lag / integrity break / metadata drift) | Video ID / Page | Evidence (tracker side / platform side, dated) | Age | Severity | Proposed repair |

**Stalled plans** - overdue scheduled pages, most-overdue first, each distinguishing no-video vs unlinked-video.

**Integrity breaks** - published pages with unavailable videos, connector response quoted.

**Coverage note** - exceptions excluded, weak-key (title-similarity) matches marked, any one-sided sweep declared.

**Run summary**

| Step | App | Produced | Status |

## Do

- Diff on video ID as the strong key; mark title-similarity matches as weak evidence
- Separate "video was never made" from "video exists but was never linked" - different owners fix them
- Rank stalls by days overdue and show carried-over age on repeat findings
- Quote connector responses verbatim for unavailable videos

## Don't

- Don't fix, sync, or relink anything mid-check - diagnosis and repair are separate approvals
- Don't treat a rescheduled plan as a stall; honor the grace period and the parked-page exceptions
- Don't assume a missing video was deleted - private, unlisted, and mistyped IDs look identical from outside
- Don't present a one-sided sweep (one connector down) as a completed check

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Both directions were swept: plan→publish stalls and publish→track gaps, plus integrity breaks
- [ ] Every finding carries page link and video ID with dates from both sides, or is labeled a hypothesis
- [ ] Stalls honor the grace period and known exceptions
- [ ] Zero writes occurred in either app
- [ ] Run summary table shows step, app, produced artifact, status for the whole run
```
